items.q: I go home every evening.
items.explanation: 毎晩 (every evening) + 帰ります (go home). 帰る means 'to return home.' 毎朝 means 'every morning.'

items.q: I make breakfast every morning.
items.explanation: 毎朝 (every morning) + 朝ごはん (breakfast) + を (object) + 作ります (make). 晩ごはん is dinner, not breakfast.

items.q: I use this every day.
items.explanation: 毎日 (every day) + これ (this) + を (object marker) + 使います (use). を marks the thing being used.

items.q: I get up at 6 AM.
items.explanation: 午前 (AM) + 六時 (6 o'clock) + に (time marker) + 起きます (get up). 午後 means PM.

items.q: I walk to the station.
items.explanation: 駅 + まで (up to/as far as) + 歩きます (walk). まで emphasizes the distance walked. に marks destination, but まで + 歩く = 'walk all the way to.'

items.q: I like red.
items.explanation: 赤 (red) + が (subject with 好き) + 好き (like) + です. 好き takes が, not は, to mark what is liked. 青 means 'blue.'

items.q: This tea is delicious.
items.explanation: この + お茶 (tea) + は (topic) + おいしい (delicious) + です. 暗い means 'dark' — not related to taste.

items.q: Lunch and dinner are the same.
items.explanation: 昼ごはん (lunch) + と (and) + 晩ごはん (dinner) + は (topic) + 同じ (same) + です.

items.q: It is dark outside.
items.explanation: 外 (outside) + は (topic) + 暗い (dark) + です. 暗い is an i-adjective meaning 'dark.'

items.q: I do not like the black one.
items.explanation: 黒い (black) + の (nominalizer, 'the one') + は (topic) + 好きではないです (do not like). の after an adjective = 'the [adjective] one.'

items.q: My older brother lives in Tokyo.
items.explanation: 兄 (older brother) + は (topic) + 東京 + に (location with 住む) + 住んでいます (is living). 住む takes に, not で. 弟 means 'younger brother.'

items.q: My younger sister is at school.
items.explanation: 妹 (younger sister) + は + 学校 + に (location of existence) + います. 姉 means 'older sister.'

items.q: That town is far.
items.explanation: あの + 町 (town) + は (topic) + 遠い (far) + です. 近い (near) is the opposite.

items.q: Where do you live?
items.explanation: どこ (where) + に + 住んでいます + か. 住む always takes に for the place of residence.

items.q: My family is my parents, my older sister, and me.
items.explanation: 家族 (family) + は + 親 (parents) + と (and) + 姉 (older sister) + と + 私 (I/me) + です. と lists members exhaustively.

items.q: The movie starts at 7 PM.
items.explanation: 映画 (movie) + は + 午後 (PM) + 七時 + に (time) + 始まります (starts, intransitive). 終わります means 'ends' — the opposite.

items.q: I took a photo of the world map.
items.explanation: 世界 (world) + の + 地図 (map) + の + 写真 (photo) + を (object) + とりました (took). Chained の connects: world → map → photo.

items.q: I went on a trip by myself.
items.explanation: 自分 (oneself) + で (by means of, 'by myself') + 旅行 (trip) + に (purpose/destination) + 行きました. 自分で = 'by oneself.'

items.q: That movie theater is close to the station.
items.explanation: あの + 映画館 (movie theater) + は + 駅 + に/から (near to) + 近い + です. Both 駅に近い and 駅から近い are natural. 遠い (far) is the opposite.

items.q: The English movie ended at 9 PM.
items.explanation: 英語 (English) + の + 映画 + は + 午後 + 九時 + に (time) + 終わりました (ended). 始まりました means 'started.'

items.q: My head hurts since this morning.
items.explanation: 今朝 (this morning) + から (since) + 頭 (head) + が (subject) + いたい (hurts) + です. から marks the starting time point.

items.q: That singer has a good voice.
items.explanation: あの + 歌手 (singer) + は (topic) + 声 (voice) + が (subject within topic) + いい (good) + です. Double-は avoidance: は marks the singer, が marks voice within that topic.

items.q: I listen to music at the company early in the morning.
items.explanation: 朝 (morning) + 早く (early, adverbial form of 早い) + 会社 + で (location of action) + 音楽 (music) + を + 聞きます (listen). Time and place phrases can swap.

items.q: Work ends at 6 PM.
items.explanation: 仕事 (work) + は + 午後 + 六時 + に (time) + 終わります (ends). 始まります means 'starts' — wrong verb.

items.q: I have a strong body and a light heart.
items.explanation: 体 (body) + が + 強くて (strong + te-form to connect) + 心 (heart/mind) + が + 軽い (light) + です. i-adj te-form: 強い → 強くて. 弱くて means 'weak.'

items.q: Please go to the hospital quickly.
items.explanation: 早く (quickly) + 病院 (hospital) + に (destination) + 行ってください (please go). 早く is the adverbial form of 早い.

items.q: That doctor is famous in this town.
items.explanation: あの + 医者 (doctor) + は + この + 町 + で (scope/context marker) + 有名 (famous) + です. で marks the scope within which the doctor is famous.

items.q: Please look at this map and answer the question.
items.explanation: この + 地図 (map) + を + 見て (te-form of 見る) + 質問 (question) + に (target) + 答えてください (please answer). Te-form connects sequential actions.

items.q: The problem is not easy to explain.
items.explanation: この + 問題 (problem) + は + 説明 (explanation) + が (subject within topic) + むずかしい (difficult) + です. The pattern Xは Yが adj = 'as for X, Y is adj.'

items.q: The dog is running quickly and strongly.
items.explanation: 犬 (dog) + が (subject) + 早く (quickly) + 強く (strongly) + 走っています (is running). Both adverbs modify the running.

items.q: You may not sell these goods.
items.explanation: この + 品物 (goods) + を + 売っては (selling +は) + いけません (must not). てはいけない = 'must not' (G23). もいいです would mean 'may' — the opposite.

items.q: You may borrow this pen to write kanji.
items.explanation: 漢字 (kanji) + を + 書くために (in order to write) + この + ペン + を + 借りてもいいです (may borrow). ために = 'in order to' (G19). てもいいです = 'it's okay to' (G23).

items.q: I tried the difficult test yesterday.
items.explanation: きのう (yesterday) + むずかしい (difficult) + 試験 (test/exam) + を (object) + 受けました (took/received). 試験を受ける = 'to take a test.'

items.q: Please do not carry heavy things.
items.explanation: 重い (heavy) + 物 (things) + を + 持たないでください (please don't carry). ない + でください = negative request. 軽い (light) is the opposite of 重い.

items.q: The classroom is on the left side of the entrance.
items.explanation: 教室 (classroom) + は + 入り口 (entrance) + の + 左 (left) + に + あります. 右 (right) is the opposite direction.

items.q: I think that answer is wrong.
items.explanation: あの + 答え (answer) + は + 悪い (bad/wrong) + と (quotation) + 思います (I think). と quotes the thought. 正しい means 'correct' — the opposite of 悪い here.

items.q: I have been to that special place once.
segments: 行ったことがあります
items.explanation: あの + 特別な (special, na-adj) + 場所 (place) + に (destination) + 一度 (once) + 行ったことがあります (have been, experience form G27).

items.q: Please think about the correct answer.
items.explanation: 正しい (correct) + 答え (answer) + を (object) + 考えてください (please think about). 考える takes を for the object of thought.

items.q: I think this way of thinking is interesting.
items.explanation: この + 考え方 (way of thinking) + は + おもしろい (interesting) + と + 思います. 考え方 = 考え (thought) + 方 (way).

items.q: I collected special items and brought them.
items.explanation: 特別な + 物 + を + 集めて (te-form of 集める, collect) + 持ってきました (brought, てくる G24). Te-form chains two actions; てくる adds directional meaning ('brought here').

items.q: The dog was washed by the owner.
items.explanation: 犬 + は (topic) + 主人 (owner) + に (by, passive agent) + 洗われました (was washed, passive of 洗う G28). に marks who performed the action in passive.

items.q: Please stop here and open the door.
items.explanation: ここ + で (location of action) + 止まって (te-form of 止まる, stop) + ドア + を + 開けてください (please open). Te-form chains two sequential actions.

items.q: The forest has become dark.
items.explanation: 森 (forest) + が (subject) + 暗くなりました (became dark). i-adj + くなる = 'become [adjective]' (G31). 暗い → 暗く + なる.

items.q: The citizens were made to walk to the station by the mayor.
items.explanation: 市民 (citizens) + は + 市長 (mayor) + に (by) + 駅 + まで (up to) + 歩かされました (were made to walk, causative-passive of 歩く G29). に marks the causer.

items.q: I think it would be convenient if the bus stop were closer.
items.explanation: バス停 (bus stop) + が + もっと (more) + 近ければ (if close, conditional of 近い G25) + 便利だ (convenient) + と + 思います. 不便 means 'inconvenient' — the opposite.
